title: Attributing Past Carbon Fluxes to CO2 and Climate Change: Respiration Response to CO2 Fertilization Shifts Regional Distribution of the Carbon Sink
justification: In particular, the Carbon Monitoring System Flux (CMS-Flux)—an atmospheric inversion estimate of net biosphere exchange using satellite observations of atmospheric concentra- tions of CO2—provided information on the carbon balance of the terrestrial ecosystem and constrained multiple aspects of the model (Liu et al., 2017, 2021).

title: Impact of Prior Terrestrial Carbon Fluxes on Simulations of Atmospheric CO2 Concentrations
justification: We assessed the impact of terrestrial fluxes derived from different biosphere models in the Multi‐scale Synthesis and Terrestrial Model Intercomparison Project (MsTMIP) on atmospheric CO2 estimations based on a global chemical transport model (GEOS‐Chem) when these fluxes were applied as prior information. ...
